text:
Once upon a time, in a quiet little town nestled among whispering woods, there lived a boy named Oliver. Oliver wasn't the tallest or the boldest, but he possessed one remarkable talent—the ability to see tiny things that others missed. His gift hadn't been bestowed upon him magically; it was simply his eyes. Oliver could look at a surface and see the intricate network of connections and tiny beings that made it tick.

His gift, though, was not without its challenges. Oliver could easily get overwhelmed by the tiny wonders he saw. Cities and forests felt crowded; his favorite places were the quiet corners of his attic, filled with antique bugs and dust motes. But his ability earned him respect, even admiration, from the adults around him; they admired his curiosity and diligence.

One particular day, under the glow of a full moon, Oliver set out to find something truly extraordinary. He wandered through the quiet meadows, noting the flutter of butterflies and the drip of raindrops forming perfect
